2605.18025.title: TeleCom-Bench: How Far Are Large Language Models from Industrial Telecommunication Applications?
2605.18025.paper_summary: While Large Language Models have achieved remarkable integration in various vertical scenarios, their deployment in the telecommunications domain remains exploratory due to the lack of a standardized evaluation framework. Current telecom benchmarks primarily focus on static, foundational knowledge and isolated atomic skills, neglecting the equipment-specific documentation and end-to-end industrial workflows essential for real-world production systems. To bridge this gap, we present TeleCom-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark comprising 12 evaluation sets with 22,678 curated samples, which evaluates LLMs across a synergistic hierarchy: (1) Multi-dimensional Knowledge Comprehension, which integrates telecommunication fundamentals, 3GPP protocols, and 5G network architecture with proprietary product knowledge across wired, core, and wireless networks via knowledge graph-driven synthesis; and (2)End-to-End Knowledge Application, which formalizes six core tasks on authentic trajectories from live network agent workflows, including intent recognition, entity extraction, event verification, tool invocation, root cause analysis, and solution generation-across network optimization and fault maintenance scenarios. Evaluations of eight state-of-the-art LLMs reveal a universal Execution Wall: while models achieve 90% accuracy in linguistic interface tasks such as intent recognition and entity extraction, performance collapses to approximately 30% in procedural execution tasks like solution generation. This capability gap demonstrates that current LLMs function competently as diagnosticians but fail as field engineers. TeleCom-Bench provides standardized diagnostics to precisely pinpoint this deficit, offering actionable guidance for domain-specific alignment toward production-ready telecom agents. The dataset and evaluation code have been released at https://github.com/ZTE-AICloud/TeleCom-Bench.
2605.18025.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在各種垂直場景中已實現顯著整合，但由於缺乏標準化的評估框架，其在電信領域的部署仍然處於探索階段。當前的電信基準主要集中在靜態的基礎知識和孤立的原子技能上，忽略了特定設備的文檔和對於實際生產系統至關重要的端到端工業工作流程。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了TeleCom-Bench，一個包含12個評估集和22,678個精心策劃樣本的綜合基準，該基準在協同層次上評估LLMs：（1）多維知識理解，整合了電信基本原理、3GPP協議和5G網絡架構，以及通過知識圖驅動的綜合在有線、核心和無線網絡中的專有產品知識；（2）端到端知識應用，對來自實時網絡代理工作流程的真實軌跡上的六個核心任務進行形式化，包括意圖識別、實體提取、事件驗證、工具調用、根本原因分析和解決方案生成——涵蓋網絡優化和故障維護場景。對八個最先進的LLMs的評估揭示了一個普遍的執行壁壘：儘管模型在意圖識別和實體提取等語言界面任務中達到90%的準確率，但在解決方案生成等程序執行任務中的表現卻下降到約30%。這一能力差距顯示當前的LLMs作為診斷者運作良好，但作為現場工程師卻失敗了。TeleCom-Bench提供標準化的診斷，以精確定位這一缺陷，並為針對生產就緒電信代理的領域特定對齊提供可行的指導。數據集和評估代碼已在https://github.com/ZTE-AICloud/TeleCom-Bench上發布。
2605.18025.author: Jieting Xiao et.al.
2605.18025.authors: Jieting Xiao, Yun Lin, Huizhen Qiu, Rui Ma, Chen Zhong, Dongyang Xu, Xiao Long, Chaoyu Zhang, Qiaobo Hao, Ding Zou, Zhiguo Yang, Yanqin Gao, Fang Tan
